generatedSentence: The venerable dynasty, designed by ancient guardians, maintained a pantheon of past victories, yet an untypical caretaker dreamt of an opportunity beyond the infirmary. Known for his kung fu prowess and a sticky muffin recipe, he often scavenged for old joypads, oils, and javelin fragments, unaware of the treachery twisted within their dynamos; his only solace was watching manatees, while a hidden submarine held a grimoire, an item for which he sought a getaway ticket. This likable caretaker, burdened by vices and the drama of his occidental royalty, felt the impulse to connect with a mythical ancestry, having hooked himself on stories of impossible achievement. Wagers made over gambling, goblet in hand, led to a dangerous bluff, as envy and ambition, like vectors, pushed him toward necromancy. A makeshift kitchen became his workshop, where he designed a hovercraft, while loyalties to his fellowships trembled, knowing his quest for the grimoire meant diving overboard into forbidden affairs, a true interlude from his worldly reality. The beast, a gelatinous horror from myths, emerged from the jungles, its number of eyes shifting, its tall form demoniacal, causing him to tremble, as he reached for a scimitar and a hatchet. A constant caffeine drip and the rhythm of pentameter on his phones couldn't calm his nerves, but he knew this climax demanded action, so he launched a haymaker, casting a net-like smaller magnet to assist him. He saw reality warp, a tiffany glow revealing coffins, realizing his loyalties were to his own survival, fighting the beast, an attribute of his will. After the fights, an exceptional tranquility settled over the roadway, the kingfisher's flight a vivid contrast to the grim package of his past. With his bowler hat askew, he found peace in walking, no longer overpacking his burdens, the krill-rich waters near Galloway a testament to a new life. His memory, like keywords on a radios dial, played fragments of influenza, but he knew the Roman kingfisher, his new spirit guide, offered a fresh start, free from the old dynasty's grip and the antimony of his past, his thumb now firmly on the path to a quieter future.
panelSentences: The venerable dynasty, designed by ancient guardians, maintained a pantheon of past victories, yet an untypical caretaker dreamt of an opportunity beyond the infirmary. Known for his kung fu prowess and a sticky muffin recipe, he often scavenged for old joypads, oils, and javelin fragments, unaware of the treachery twisted within their dynamos; his only solace was watching manatees, while a hidden submarine held a grimoire, an item for which he sought a getaway ticket.

This likable caretaker, burdened by vices and the drama of his occidental royalty, felt the impulse to connect with a mythical ancestry, having hooked himself on stories of impossible achievement. Wagers made over gambling, goblet in hand, led to a dangerous bluff, as envy and ambition, like vectors, pushed him toward necromancy. A makeshift kitchen became his workshop, where he designed a hovercraft, while loyalties to his fellowships trembled, knowing his quest for the grimoire meant diving overboard into forbidden affairs, a true interlude from his worldly reality.

The beast, a gelatinous horror from myths, emerged from the jungles, its number of eyes shifting, its tall form demoniacal, causing him to tremble, as he reached for a scimitar and a hatchet. A constant caffeine drip and the rhythm of pentameter on his phones couldn't calm his nerves, but he knew this climax demanded action, so he launched a haymaker, casting a net-like smaller magnet to assist him. He saw reality warp, a tiffany glow revealing coffins, realizing his loyalties were to his own survival, fighting the beast, an attribute of his will.

After the fights, an exceptional tranquility settled over the roadway, the kingfisher's flight a vivid contrast to the grim package of his past. With his bowler hat askew, he found peace in walking, no longer overpacking his burdens, the krill-rich waters near Galloway a testament to a new life. His memory, like keywords on a radios dial, played fragments of influenza, but he knew the Roman kingfisher, his new spirit guide, offered a fresh start, free from the old dynasty's grip and the antimony of his past, his thumb now firmly on the path to a quieter future.
rawPanelSentences: The venerable dynasty, designed by ancient guardians, maintained a pantheon of past victories, yet an untypical caretaker dreamt of an opportunity beyond the infirmary. Known for his kung fu prowess and a sticky muffin recipe, he often scavenged for old joypads, oils, and javelin fragments, unaware of the treachery twisted within their dynamos; his only solace was watching manatees, while a hidden submarine held a grimoire, an item for which he sought a getaway ticket.

This likable caretaker, burdened by vices and the drama of his occidental royalty, felt the impulse to connect with a mythical ancestry, having hooked himself on stories of impossible achievement. Wagers made over gambling, goblet in hand, led to a dangerous bluff, as envy and ambition, like vectors, pushed him toward necromancy. A makeshift kitchen became his workshop, where he designed a hovercraft, while loyalties to his fellowships trembled, knowing his quest for the grimoire meant diving overboard into forbidden affairs, a true interlude from his worldly reality.

The beast, a gelatinous horror from myths, emerged from the jungles, its number of eyes shifting, its tall form demoniacal, causing him to tremble, as he reached for a scimitar and a hatchet. A constant caffeine drip and the rhythm of pentameter on his phones couldn't calm his nerves, but he knew this climax demanded action, so he launched a haymaker, casting a net-like smaller magnet to assist him. He saw reality warp, a tiffany glow revealing coffins, realizing his loyalties were to his own survival, fighting the beast, an attribute of his will.

After the fights, an exceptional tranquility settled over the roadway, the kingfisher's flight a vivid contrast to the grim package of his past. With his bowler hat askew, he found peace in walking, no longer overpacking his burdens, the krill-rich waters near Galloway a testament to a new life. His memory, like keywords on a radios dial, played fragments of influenza, but he knew the Roman kingfisher, his new spirit guide, offered a fresh start, free from the old dynasty's grip and the antimony of his past, his thumb now firmly on the path to a quieter future.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A grizzled, stocky man with a neatly trimmed beard, wearing a worn, patched-up jumpsuit and a high-tech wrist gauntlet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a futuristic, cluttered submarine control room, looking at an ancient grimoire with a thoughtful expression, surrounded by glowing screens and old joypads, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist in a makeshift, neon-lit kitchen, standing over a partially assembled hovercraft, looking determined while holding a shimmering goblet, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist engaged in dynamic kung fu combat with a large, gelatinous, multi-eyed beast in a bioluminescent jungle, striking with a scimitar and a hatchet, low angle, action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist walking peacefully down a futuristic roadway at sunset, a kingfisher flying overhead, his bowler hat slightly askew, a sense of calm after the storm, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A chance for advancement, progress or profit.
definitions.example: The world is full of opportunities and it's up to me to see them and pursue them.

definitions.definition: A favorable circumstance or occasion.
definitions.example: Having a holiday is a great opportunity to relax.

definitions.definition: (Euro-English) opportuneness
definitions.example: The Court questioned the opportunity of introducing these measures in such an uncertain economic climate.

definitions.definition: To turn the ends of something, usually thread, rope etc., in opposite directions, often using force.
To join together by twining one part around another.
To contort; to writhe; to complicate; to crook spirally; to convolve.
To wreathe; to wind; to encircle; to unite by intertexture of parts.
To wind into; to insinuate.
definitions.example: Avarice twists itself into all human concerns.

definitions.definition: To turn a knob etc.
To distort or change the truth or meaning of words when repeating.
To form a twist (in any of the above noun meanings).
To injure (a body part) by bending it in the wrong direction.
(of a path) To wind; to follow a bendy or wavy course; to have many bends.
To cause to rotate.
To dance the twist (a type of dance characterised by twisting one's hips).
To coax.
In the game of blackjack (pontoon or twenty-one), to be dealt another card.
Contorted.
Wound spirally.
Mentally disturbed or unsound.
definitions.example: The murders were committed by a twisted sociopath.

definitions.definition: Under the influence of multiple intoxicants, usually alcohol and marijuana.
A book of instructions in the use of alchemy or magic, especially one containing spells for summoning demons.
Someone who guards, watches over, or protects.
A person legally responsible for a minor (in loco parentis).
A person legally responsible for an incompetent person.
A superior in a Franciscan monastery.
A major or final enemy; boss.
A series of rulers or dynasts from one family.
A team or organization which has an extended period of success or dominant performance.
To plan and carry out (a picture, work of art, construction etc.).
To plan (to do something).
definitions.example: The king designed to mount an expedition to the New World.

definitions.definition: To assign, appoint (something to someone); to designate.
To mark out and exhibit; to designate; to indicate; to show; to point out; to appoint.
To manifest requirements to be satisfied by an object or process for them to solve a problem.
definitions.example: The client had me create new designs until they were satisfied with one.

definitions.definition: Created according to a design
Planned; designated.
An electricity generator, a dynamo-electric machine.
An energetic person.
definitions.example: That new Vice President of Marketing is a real dynamo; sales have already gone up this quarter.

definitions.definition: A temple dedicated to all the gods.
All the gods of a particular people or religion, particularly the ancient Greek gods residing on Olympus, considered as a group.
(by extension) A category or classification denoting the most honored persons of a group.
An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
definitions.example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definitions.definition: The condition of having succeeded in a conflict or other effort.
A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
To achieve a victory
Commanding respect because of age, dignity, character or position.
Worthy of reverence.
Ancient, antiquated or archaic.
Made sacred especially by religious or historical association.
Giving an impression of aged goodness and benevolence.
A German.
A dish made by fermenting finely chopped cabbage.
A German person.
A Chinese martial art.
A place where sick or injured people are cared for, especially a small hospital; sickhouse.
A clinic or dispensary within another institution.
To collect and remove refuse, or to search through refuse, carrion, or abandoned items for useful material
To remove unwanted material from something, especially to purify molten metal by removing impurities
To expel the exhaust gases from the cylinder of an internal combustion engine, and draw in air for the next cycle
To feed on carrion or refuse
A boat that can go underwater.
A kind of sandwich made in a long loaf of bread.
Pitch delivered with an underhand motion.
Any submarine plant or animal.
A stowaway on a seagoing vessel.
To operate or serve on a submarine.
To torpedo; to destroy with a sudden sneak attack.
(sometimes figurative) To sink or submerge oneself.
Undersea.
Hidden or undisclosed.
definitions.example: a submarine patent

definitions.definition: Of a pitch, thrown with the hand lower than the elbow.
A sticky note, such as a post-it note.
definitions.example: Her desk is covered with yellow stickies.

definitions.definition: A discussion thread fixed at the top of the list of topics or threads so as to keep it in view.
A small adhesive particle found in wastepaper.
A sweet dessert wine.
(bulletin boards) to fix a thread at the top of the list of topics or threads so as to keep it in view.
Able or likely to stick.
definitions.example: Is this tape sticky enough to stay on that surface?

definitions.definition: Potentially difficult to escape from.
definitions.example: This is a sticky situation. We could be in this for weeks if we're not careful.

definitions.definition: Of weather, hot and windless and with high humidity, so that people feel sticky from sweating.
(of a setting) Persistent.
definitions.example: We should make the printing direction sticky so the user doesn't have to keep setting it.

definitions.definition: (of a window) Appearing on all virtual desktops.
(of threads on a bulletin board) Fixed at the top of the list of topics or threads so as to keep it in view.
(of a website) Compelling enough to keep visitors from leaving.
definitions.example: A woman has come to me with the complaint that her website is not sticky: 70% of the visits last 30 seconds or less.

definitions.definition: Any of several plant-eating marine mammals, of family Trichechidae, found in tropical regions.
A type of flattish bun, usually cut in two horizontally, toasted and spread with butter, etc, before being eaten.
A type of individual bread such as corn, bran, banana or zucchini bread often sliced and spread with butter, etc before being eaten.
(especially US) (informally) A cupcake without frosting, but sometimes glazed.
A mechanism used in the Java Network Launching Protocol analogous to the cookie mechanism and which permits a program running in a browser to perform operations on a client machine.
Term of endearment.
definitions.example: I love you, muffin!

definitions.definition: (sexual slang) A vulva.
A less talented player; one who muffs, or drops the ball.
A charming, attractive young man.
A small plate, smaller than a twiffler.
A type of game controller held in the hand, where the digits (especially thumbs) are used to provide input
A light spear thrown with the hand and used as a weapon.
A metal-tipped spear thrown for distance in an athletic field event.
To pierce with a javelin.
Not typical, atypical, unusual
Liquid fat.
Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.
definitions.example: I prefer to paint in oil

definitions.definition: To lubricate with oil.
To grease with oil for cooking.
Deliberate, often calculated, disregard for trust or faith.
The act of violating the confidence of another, usually for personal gain.
Treason.
A pass entitling the holder to admission to a show, concert, etc.
A pass entitling the holder to board a train, a bus, a plane, or other means of transportation
A citation for a traffic violation.
A permit to operate a machine on a construction site.
A service request, used to track complaints or requests that an issue be handled. (Generally technical support related).
A list of candidates for an election, or a particular theme to a candidate's manifesto.
definitions.example: Joe has joined the party's ticket for the county elections.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem; something that is needed.
definitions.example: I saw my first bike as my ticket to freedom.

definitions.definition: A little note or notice.
A tradesman's bill or account (hence the phrase on ticket and eventually on tick).
A label affixed to goods to show their price or description.
A certificate or token of a share in a lottery or other scheme for distributing money, goods, etc.
A visiting card.
To issue someone a ticket, as for travel or for a violation of a local or traffic law.
To mark with a ticket.
definitions.example: to ticket goods in a retail store

definitions.definition: A means of escape.
The effecting of an escape.
A vacation or holiday, or the destination for one.
Pertaining to an escape, as in a vehicle or plans.
definitions.example: They'd been discussing their getaway plans for weeks.

definitions.definition: Jelly-like.
Of or referring to gelatin.
Capable of being liked.
definitions.example: I suppose he's likable; why shouldn't he be?

definitions.definition: (of a person) Having qualities tending to result in being liked; friendly, personable.
definitions.example: She's a naturally likable person, with lots of friends.

definitions.definition: Either of the two fleshy protrusions around the opening of the mouth.
A part of the body that resembles a lip, such as the edge of a wound or the labia.
(by extension) The projecting rim of an open container; a short open spout.
Backtalk; verbal impertinence.
definitions.example: Don’t give me any lip!

definitions.definition: The edge of a high spot of land.
The sharp cutting edge on the end of an auger.
One of the two opposite divisions of a labiate corolla.
The distinctive petal of the Orchis family.
One of the edges of the aperture of a univalve shell.
Embouchure: the condition or strength of a wind instrumentalist's lips.
To touch or grasp with the lips; to kiss; to lap the lips against (something).
(of something inanimate) To touch lightly.
To wash against a surface, lap.
To rise or flow up to or over the edge of something.
To form the rim, edge or margin of something.
To utter verbally.
To simulate speech by moving the lips without making any sound; to mouth.
To make a golf ball hit the lip of the cup, without dropping in.
To change the sound of (a musical note played on a wind instrument) by moving or tensing the lips.
A composition, normally in prose, telling a story and intended to be represented by actors impersonating the characters and speaking the dialogue
Such a work for television, radio or the cinema (usually one that is not a comedy)
Theatrical plays in general
A situation in real life that has the characteristics of such a theatrical play

Rumor, lying or exaggerated reaction to life or online events; melodrama; an angry dispute or scene; a situation made more complicated or worse than it should be; intrigue or spiteful interpersonal maneuvering.
The rank, status, power or authority of a monarch.
People of royal rank, plus their families, treated as a group.
A royal right or prerogative, such as the exploitation of a natural resource; the granting of such a right; payment received for such a right.
The payment received by an owner of real property for exploitation of mineral rights in the property.
(by extension) Payment made to a writer, composer, inventor etc for the sale or use of intellectual property, invention etc.
Someone in a privileged position.
A king and a queen as a starting hand in Texas hold 'em.
The bounds of a royal burgh.
An act of bluffing; a false expression of the strength of one's position in order to intimidate; braggadocio.
definitions.example: That is only bluff, or a bluff.

definitions.definition: An attempt to represent oneself as holding a stronger hand than one actually does.
definitions.example: John's bet was a bluff: he bet without even so much as a pair.

definitions.definition: The card game poker.
One who bluffs; a bluffer.
An excuse.
To make a bluff; to give the impression that one's hand is stronger than it is.
definitions.example: John bluffed by betting without even a pair.

definitions.definition: (by analogy) To frighten or deter with a false show of strength or confidence; to give a false impression of strength or temerity in order to intimidate and gain some advantage.
definitions.example: The government claims it will call an election if this bill does not pass. Is it truly ready to do so, or is it bluffing?

definitions.definition: To take advantage by bluffing.
definitions.example: We bluffed our way past the guards.

definitions.definition: Resentful desire of something possessed by another or others (but not limited to material possessions).
An object of envious notice or feeling.
Hatred, enmity, ill-feeling.
Emulation; rivalry.
Public odium; ill repute.
To feel displeasure or hatred towards (someone) for their good fortune or possessions.
To have envious feelings (at).
To give (something) to (someone) grudgingly or reluctantly; to begrudge.
To show malice or ill will; to rail.
To do harm to; to injure; to disparage.
To hate.
To emulate.
To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.
definitions.example: Kayaking is an Olympic sport.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.
definitions.example: On a dare, he kayaked the Harlem River in New York from Hell's Gate to Spyten Duyvil.

definitions.definition: A water sport involving racing, or doing tricks in, a kayak
A room or area for preparing food.
Cuisine.
The nape of a person's hairline, often referring to its uncombed or "nappy" look.
The percussion section of an orchestra.
A utensil for roasting meat.
A domesticated or uneducated form of a language.
A public gaming room in a casino.
Anything eaten as a relish with bread, potatoes, etc.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
To embellish a basic food; to season, add condiments, etc.
(by extension) To embellish; to dress up.
A characteristic or quality of a thing.
definitions.example: His finest attribute is his kindness.

definitions.definition: (grammar) A word that qualifies a noun, a qualifier.
definitions.example: In the clause "My jacket is more expensive than yours", "My" is the attribute of "jacket".

definitions.definition: That which is predicated or affirmed of a subject; a predicate; an accident.
An option or setting belonging to some object.
definitions.example: A file with the read-only attribute set cannot be overwritten.

definitions.definition: A semantic item with which a method or other code element may be decorated.
definitions.example: Properties can be marked as obsolete with an attribute, which will cause the compiler to generate a warning if they are used.

definitions.definition: A numeric value representing the colours of part of the screen display.
To ascribe (something) to a given cause, reason etc.
To associate ownership or authorship of (something) to someone.
definitions.example: This poem is attributed to Browning.

definitions.definition: The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
A great or heroic deed or feat; something accomplished by valor or boldness
An escutcheon or ensign armorial; now generally applied to the funeral shield commonly called hatchment.
An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.
definitions.example: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

definitions.definition: (grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in an instant.
Something deposited, laid, or hazarded on the event of a contest or an unsettled question; a bet; a stake; a pledge.
That on which bets are laid; the subject of a bet.
A contract by which two parties or more agree that a certain sum of money, or other thing, shall be paid or delivered to one of them, on the happening or not happening of an uncertain event.
An offer to make oath.
To bet something; to put it up as collateral
definitions.example: I'd wager my boots on it.

definitions.definition: To suppose; to dare say.
definitions.example: I'll wager that Johnson knows something about all this.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of wage; one who wages.
Something invented.
definitions.example: I'm afraid there was no burglar. It was all the housekeeper's invention.

definitions.definition: The act of inventing.
definitions.example: The invention of the printing press was probably the most significant innovation of the medieval ages.

definitions.definition: The capacity to invent.
definitions.example: It took quite a bit of invention to come up with a plan, but we did it.

definitions.definition: A small, self-contained composition, particularly those in J.S. Bach’s Two- and Three-part Inventions.
definitions.example: I particularly like the inventions in C-minor.

definitions.definition: The act of discovering or finding; the act of finding out; discovery.
definitions.example: That judicial method which serveth best for the invention of truth.

definitions.definition: An impossibility
definitions.example: Late 14th century: “Madame,” quod he, “this were an impossible!” — Geoffrey Chaucer, ‘The Franklin's Tale’, Canterbury Tales

definitions.definition: Not possible; not able to be done or happen.
definitions.example: It is difficult, if not impossible, to memorize 20,000 consecutive numbers.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Very difficult to deal with.
definitions.example: You never listen to a word I say – you're impossible!

definitions.definition: Imaginary
definitions.example: impossible quantities, or imaginary numbers

definitions.definition: A distinct physical object.
definitions.example: Tweezers are great for manipulating small items.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object that can be picked up for later use.
A line of text having a legal or other meaning; a separate particular in an account.
definitions.example: In response to the first item, we deny all wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: (psychometrics) A question on a test, which may include its answers.
definitions.example: The exam has 100 items, each of which includes a correct response and three distractors.

definitions.definition: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
definitions.example: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

definitions.definition: Two people who are having a relationship with each other.
definitions.example: Jack and Jill are an item.

definitions.definition: A short article in a newspaper.
definitions.example: an item concerning the weather

definitions.definition: A hint; an innuendo.
To make a note of.
Likewise
The act or ability to simulate the appearance of someone or something else.
definitions.example: They say that mimicry is the sincerest form of flattery, but I still think I'm being mocked when he acts just like me.

definitions.definition: To attach a hook to.
definitions.example: Hook the bag here, and the conveyor will carry it away.

definitions.definition: To catch with a hook (hook a fish).
definitions.example: He hooked a snake accidentally, and was so scared he dropped his rod into the water.

definitions.definition: To work yarn into a fabric using a hook; to crochet.
To insert in a curved way reminiscent of a hook.
definitions.example: He hooked his fingers through his belt loops.

definitions.definition: To ensnare or obligate someone, as if with a hook.
definitions.example: A free trial is a good way to hook customers.

definitions.definition: To steal.
To connect (hook into, hook together).
definitions.example: If you hook your network cable into the jack, you'll be on the network.

definitions.definition: (usually in passive) To make addicted; to captivate.
definitions.example: He had gotten hooked on cigarettes in his youth.

definitions.definition: To play a hook shot.
To succeed in heeling the ball back out of a scrum (used particularly of the team's designated hooker).
To engage in the illegal maneuver of hooking (i.e., using the hockey stick to trip or block another player)
definitions.example: The opposing team's forward hooked me, but the referee didn't see it, so no penalty.

definitions.definition: To swerve a ball; kick a ball so it swerves or bends.
To engage in prostitution.
definitions.example: I had a cheap flat in the bad part of town, and I could watch the working girls hooking from my bedroom window.

definitions.definition: (Scrabble) To play a word perpendicular to another word by adding a single letter to the existing word.
To finesse.
To seize or pierce with the points of the horns, as cattle in attacking enemies; to gore.
To move or go with a sudden turn.
Having a sharp curve at the end; resembling a hook.
definitions.example: a hooked nose

definitions.definition: Addicted; unable to resist or cease doing.
definitions.example: He will never leave her because he is hooked on her chocolate chip cookies.

definitions.definition: A company of people that share the same interest or aim.
definitions.example: The Fellowship of the Ring

definitions.definition: Company, companions; a group of people or things following another.
A feeling of friendship, relatedness or connection between people.
A merit-based scholarship.
A temporary position at an academic institution with limited teaching duties and ample time for research; this may also be called a postdoc.
A period of supervised, sub-specialty medical training in the United States and Canada that a physician may undertake after completing a specialty training program or residency.
The proportional division of profit and loss among partners.
A container intended to hold smaller containers.
To pack too much.
A Western Christian of the Latin rite
Of, pertaining to, or situated in, the occident, or west; western.
definitions.example: occidental climates, or customs; an occidental planet.

definitions.definition: Of a gem or precious stone: of inferior value or quality.
To throw over the edge of a boat into the water.
Outside of a boat, in the water
Over the edge; especially, off or outside of a boat.
definitions.example: It was their practice to throw the scraps overboard.

definitions.definition: Excessively; too much.
definitions.example: They really went overboard with the party preparations.

definitions.definition: The state of organisms preceding their death, characterized by biological processes such as metabolism and reproduction and distinguishing them from inanimate objects; the state of being alive and living.
definitions.example: Having experienced both, the vampire decided that he preferred (un)death to life.  He gave up on life.

definitions.definition: The animating principle or force that keeps an inorganic thing or concept metaphorically alive (dynamic, relevant, etc) and makes it a "living document", "living constitution", etc.
Lifeforms, generally or collectively.
definitions.example: It's life, but not as we know it.   She discovered plant life on the planet.   The rover discovered signs of life on the alien world.

definitions.definition: A living individual; the fact of a particular individual being alive. (Chiefly when indicating individuals were lost (died) or saved.)
definitions.example: Many lives were lost during the war.   Her quick thinking saved many dogs' lives.

definitions.definition: Existence.
definitions.example: Man's life on this planet has been marked by continual conflict.   the eternal life of the soul

definitions.definition: A period of time during which something has existence.
Animation; spirit; vivacity.
A biography.
definitions.example: His life of the founder is finished, except for the title.

definitions.definition: Nature, reality, and the forms that exist in it.
An opportunity for existence.
The life insurance industry.
definitions.example: I work in life.

definitions.definition: A life assured under a life assurance policy (equivalent to the policy itself for a single life contract).
Someone who takes care of a place or thing; someone looking after a place, or responsible for keeping it in good repair.
Someone who takes care of a person; a parent, carer or other guardian.
Temporary, on a short term basis.
definitions.example: Johnson had to be drafted in as the caretaker manager after Hewlett resigned without warning the day before the final.

definitions.definition: A rectangular closed box in which the body of a dead person is placed for burial.
The eighth Lenormand card.
A basket.
A casing or crust, or a mold, of pastry, as for a pie.
A conical paper bag, used by grocers.
The hollow crust or hoof of a horse's foot, below the coronet, in which is the coffin bone.
A storage container for nuclear waste.
To place in a coffin.
Something which is packed, a parcel, a box, an envelope.
Something which consists of various components, such as a piece of computer software.
definitions.example: Did you test the software package to ensure completeness?

definitions.definition: A piece of software which has been prepared in such a way that it can be installed with a package manager.
The act of packing something.
Something resembling a package.
A package holiday.
A football formation.
definitions.example: For third and short, they're going to bring in their jumbo package.

definitions.definition: The male genitalia.
A charge made for packing goods.
A group of related stories spread over several pages.
To pack or bundle something.
To travel on a package holiday.
To prepare (a book, a television series, etc.), including all stages from research to production, in order to sell the result to a publisher or broadcaster.
A way used as a road.
The main or central portion of a road, used by the vehicles.
The portion of a bridge or railway used by traffic.
One who engages in the sport of bowling.
The player currently bowling.
A player selected mainly for his bowling ability.
(1800s) The pitcher.
An exception, or something having an exceptional value
Forming an exception; not ordinary; uncommon; rare.
definitions.example: What an exceptional flower!

definitions.definition: Better than the average; superior due to exception or rarity.
definitions.example: The quality of the beer was exceptional.

definitions.definition: Corresponding to something of lower dimension under a birational correspondence.
definitions.example: an exceptional curve; an exceptional divisor

definitions.definition: A bad habit.
definitions.example: Gluttony is a vice, not a virtue.

definitions.definition: Any of various crimes related (depending on jurisdiction) to prostitution, pornography, gambling, alcohol, or drugs.
A defect in the temper or behaviour of a horse, such as to make the animal dangerous, to injure its health, or to diminish its usefulness.
A mechanical screw apparatus used for clamping or holding (also spelled vise).
A tool for drawing lead into cames, or flat grooved rods, for casements.
A grip or grasp.
A winding or spiral staircase.
One who acts in place of a superior.
A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.
definitions.example: The vectors in {\mathbb Q}[X] are the single-variable polynomials with rational coefficients: one is \textstyle x^{42}+\frac1{137}x-1.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.
definitions.example: a vector image

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
To look at, see, or view for a period of time.
definitions.example: I'm tired of watching TV.

definitions.definition: To observe over a period of time; to notice or pay attention.
definitions.example: Put a little baking soda in some vinegar and watch what happens.

definitions.definition: To mind, attend, or guard.
definitions.example: He has to watch the kids that afternoon.

definitions.definition: To be wary or cautious of.
definitions.example: You should watch that guy. He has a reputation for lying.

definitions.definition: To attend to dangers to or regarding.
definitions.example: Watch what you say.

definitions.definition: To remain awake with a sick or dying person; to maintain a vigil.
To be vigilant or on one's guard.
definitions.example: For some must watch, while some must sleep: So runs the world away.

definitions.definition: To act as a lookout.
(of a buoy) To serve the purpose of a watchman by floating properly in its place.
To be awake.
To be on the lookout for; to wait for expectantly.
The act of one who watches.
A short blow or gust.
Something small or insignificant; a trifle.
A fife or small flute.
To blow a short gust.
To waffle, talk aimlessly.
To waste time.
To travel quickly with an accompanying wind-like sound; whizz, whistle along.
(of a bird) To descend rapidly from a height once the decision to land has been made, involving fast side-slipping first one way and then the other.
To waver, or shake, as if moved by gusts of wind; to shift, turn, or veer about.
To wave or shake quickly; to cause to whiffle.
To change from one opinion or course to another; to use evasions; to prevaricate; to be fickle.
To disperse with, or as with, a whiff, or puff; to scatter.

A traditional story which embodies a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; a sacred narrative regarding a god, a hero, the origin of the world or of a people, etc.
Such stories as a genre.
definitions.example: Myth was the product of man's emotion and imagination, acted upon by his surroundings. (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, cited after OED)

definitions.definition: A commonly-held but false belief, a common misconception; a fictitious or imaginary person or thing; a popular conception about a real person or event which exaggerates or idealizes reality.
definitions.example: Scientists debunk the myth that gum stays in the human stomach for seven years.

definitions.definition: A person or thing held in excessive or quasi-religious awe or admiration based on popular legend
definitions.example: Father Flanagan was legendary, his institution an American myth. (Tucson (Arizona) Citizen, 20 September 1979, 5A/3, cited after OED)

definitions.definition: A person or thing existing only in imagination, or whose actual existence is not verifiable.
A temporary (usually insubstantial) substitution.
Made to work or suffice; improvised; substituted.
definitions.example: They used the ledge and a few branches for a makeshift shelter.

definitions.definition: A small light axe with a short handle; a tomahawk.
To cut with a hatchet.
A vehicle supported on a cushion of air, able to traverse many different types of terrain and travel over water, used for transport.
A person or machine which harvests or prepares tall grass for use as animal fodder.
(fisticuffs) A particularly powerful punch, especially one which knocks down an opponent, thrown like a scythe chop for cutting hay, as agricultural haymakers used to have strong arms.
(by extension) Any decisive blow, shock, or forceful action.
A kind of gauze, or very thin silk.
(possibly nonstandard) Someone or something that is tall.
(of a person) Having a vertical extent greater than the average. For example, somebody with a height of over 6 feet would generally be considered to be tall.
definitions.example: Being tall is an advantage in basketball.

definitions.definition: (of a building, etc.) Having its top a long way up; having a great vertical (and often greater than horizontal) extent; high.
(of a story) Hard to believe, such as a tall story or a tall tale.
(of a cup of coffee) A cup of coffee smaller than grande, usually 8 ounces.
Obsequious; obedient.
Seemly; suitable; fitting, becoming, comely; attractive, handsome.
Bold; brave; courageous; valiant.
Fine; proper; admirable; great; excellent.
A shake, quiver, or vibration.
To shake, quiver, or vibrate.
definitions.example: Her lip started to tremble as she burst into tears

definitions.definition: To fear; to be afraid.
(physical) To move, or be moved, away.
To direct (one's eyes, gaze etc.).
To add up (a column of figures, accounts etc.); cross-cast refers to adding up a row of figures.
(social) To predict, to decide, to plan.
To perform, bring forth (a magical spell or enchantment).
To throw (light etc.) on or upon something, or in a given direction.
To give birth to (a child) prematurely; to miscarry.
To shape (molten metal etc.) by pouring into a mould; to make (an object) in such a way.
To twist or warp (of fabric, timber etc.).
To bring the bows of a sailing ship on to the required tack just as the anchor is weighed by use of the headsail; to bring (a ship) round.
To deposit (a ballot or voting paper); to formally register (one's vote).
To change a variable type from, for example, integer to real, or integer to text.
definitions.example: Casting is generally an indication of bad design.

definitions.definition: Of dogs, hunters: to spread out and search for a scent.
To set (a bone etc.) in a cast.
To open a circle in order to begin a spell or meeting of witches.
To broadcast.
definitions.example: The streamer was the first to cast footage of the new game.

definitions.definition: (of an object) To join (to another object): to attach, or to be intended to attach or capable of attaching, to another object.
definitions.example: I think this piece connects to that piece over there.

definitions.definition: (of two objects) To join: to attach, or to be intended to attach or capable of attaching, to each other.
definitions.example: Both roads have the same name, but they don't connect: they're on opposite sides of the river, and there's no bridge there.

definitions.definition: (of an object) To join (two other objects), or to join (one object) to (another object): to be a link between two objects, thereby attaching them to each other.
definitions.example: The new railroad will connect the northern part of the state to the southern part.

definitions.definition: (of a person) To join (two other objects), or to join (one object) to (another object): to take one object and attach it to another.
definitions.example: I connected the printer to the computer, but I couldn't get it work.

definitions.definition: To join an electrical or telephone line to a circuit or network.
definitions.example: When the technician connects my house, I'll be able to access the internet.

definitions.definition: To associate; to establish a relation between.
definitions.example: I didn't connect my lost jewelry with the news of an area cat burglar until the police contacted me.

definitions.definition: To make a travel connection; to switch from one means of transport to another as part of the same trip.
definitions.example: I'm flying to London where I connect with a flight heading to Hungary.

definitions.definition: An alkaloid, C8H10N4O2, found naturally in tea and coffee plants which acts as a mild stimulant on the central nervous system.
Pertaining to, characteristic of, or produced by a demon or evil spirit; devilish or fiendish.
A line in a poem having five metrical feet.
Poetic metre in which each line has five feet.
A device for transmitting conversations and other sounds in real time across distances, now often a small portable unit also capable of running software etc.
To call (someone) using a telephone.
A speech segment that possesses distinct physical or perceptual properties, considered as a physical event without regard to its place in the phonology of a language.
Headphones
The state of being actual or real.
definitions.example: The reality of the crash scene on TV dawned upon him only when he saw the victim was no actor but his friend.

definitions.definition: A real entity, event or other fact.
definitions.example: The ultimate reality of life is that it ends in death.

definitions.definition: The entirety of all that is real.
An individual observer's own subjective perception of that which is real.
Loyalty; devotion.
Realty; real estate.
(often in the plural) Something which is done or is to be done; business of any kind, commercial, professional, or public.
definitions.example: a difficult affair to manage

definitions.definition: Any proceeding or action which it is wished to refer to or characterize vaguely.
definitions.example: an affair of honor, a duel;  an affair of love, an intrigue

definitions.definition: An action or engagement not of sufficient magnitude to be called a battle.
A material object (vaguely designated).
definitions.example: He used a hook-shaped affair with a long handle to unlock the car.

definitions.definition: An adulterous relationship. (from affaire de cœur).
A romantic relationship with someone who is not one's regular partner (boyfriend, girlfriend).
A person with whom someone has an adulterous relationship.
A party or social gathering, especially of a formal nature.
The (male or female) genitals.
(now uncommon or literary) To wish, desire (something).
definitions.example: Do what you will.

definitions.definition: (nowadays rare) To wish or desire (that something happen); to intend (that).
(auxiliary) To habitually do (a given action).
(auxiliary) To choose to (do something); used to express intention but without any temporal connotations (+ bare infinitive), often in negation.
definitions.example: I’ve told him three times, but he won’t take his medicine.

definitions.definition: (auxiliary) Used to express the future tense, sometimes with some implication of volition when used in the first person. Compare shall.
(auxiliary) To be able to, to have the capacity to.
definitions.example: Unfortunately, only one of these gloves will actually fit over my hand.

definitions.definition: (auxiliary) Expressing a present tense with some conditional or subjective weakening: "will turn out to", "must by inference".
Concerned with human or earthly matters, physical as opposed to spiritual.
Concerned with secular rather than sacred matters.
Sophisticated, especially because of surfeit; versed in the ways of the world.
In a worldly manner.
An intervening episode, etc.
An entertainment between the acts of a play.
A short piece put between the parts of a longer composition.
To provide with an interlude.
An acute contagious disease of the upper airways and lungs, caused by a virus, which rapidly spreads around the world in seasonal epidemics.
A thrust; a push; a sudden force that impels.
A wish or urge, particularly a sudden one prompting action.
definitions.example: The impulse to learn drove me to study night and day.

definitions.definition: The integral of force over time.
definitions.example: The total impulse from the impact will depend on the kinetic energy of the bullet.

definitions.definition: To impel; to incite.
A sword of Persian origin that features a curved blade.
A long-handled billhook.
To strike or slice with, or as if with, a scimitar.
Not large or big; insignificant; few in number.
definitions.example: A small group.

definitions.definition: Young, as a child.
definitions.example: Remember when the children were small?

definitions.definition: (writing, incomparable) Minuscule or lowercase, referring to written or printed letters.
Envincing little worth or ability; not large-minded; paltry; mean.
Not prolonged in duration; not extended in time; short.
definitions.example: a small space of time

definitions.definition: Slender, gracefully slim.
In a small fashion.
In or into small pieces.
To a small extent.
In a low tone; softly.
A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
definitions.example: Zero, one, −1, 2.5, and pi are all numbers.

definitions.definition: A numeral: a symbol for a non-negative integer.
definitions.example: The number 8 is usually made with a single stroke.

definitions.definition: An element of one of several sets: natural numbers, integers, rational numbers, real numbers, complex numbers, and sometimes extensions such as hypercomplex numbers, etc.
definitions.example: The equation e^{i\pi}+1=0 includes the most important numbers: 1, 0, \pi, i, and e.

definitions.definition: (Followed by a numeral; used attributively) Indicating the position of something in a list or sequence. Abbreviations: No or No., no or no. (in each case, sometimes written with a superscript "o", like Nº or №). The symbol "#" is also used in this manner.
definitions.example: Horse number 5 won the race.

definitions.definition: Quantity.
definitions.example: Any number of people can be reading from a given repository at a time.

definitions.definition: A sequence of digits and letters used to register people, automobiles, and various other items.
definitions.example: Her passport number is C01X864TN.

definitions.definition: A telephone number.
(grammar) Of a word or phrase, the state of being singular, dual or plural, shown by inflection.
definitions.example: Adjectives and nouns should agree in gender, number, and case.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Poetic metres; verses, rhymes.
A performance; especially, a single song or song and dance routine within a larger show.
definitions.example: For his second number, he sang "The Moon Shines Bright".

definitions.definition: A person.
An item of clothing, particularly a stylish one.
A marijuana cigarette, or joint; also, a quantity of marijuana bought form a dealer.
An issue of a periodical publication.
definitions.example: the latest number of a magazine

definitions.definition: A large amount, in contrast to a smaller amount; numerical preponderance.
To label (items) with numbers; to assign numbers to (items).
definitions.example: Number the baskets so that we can find them easily.

definitions.definition: To total or count; to amount to.
definitions.example: I don’t know how many books are in the library, but they must number in the thousands.

definitions.definition: Divination involving the dead or death.
Loosely, any sorcery or witchcraft, especially involving death or the dead, particularly sorcery involving raising or reanimating the dead.
Synonym of necroposting
To contend in physical conflict, either singly or in war, battle etc.
definitions.example: A wounded animal will fight like a maniac, relentless, savage and murderous.

definitions.definition: To contend in physical conflict with each other, either singly or in war, battle etc.
definitions.example: The two boxers have been fighting for more than half an hour.

definitions.definition: To strive for something; to campaign or contend for success.
definitions.example: He fought for the Democrats in the last election.

definitions.definition: To conduct or engage in (battle, warfare etc.).
definitions.example: The battle was fought just over that hill.

definitions.definition: To engage in combat with; to oppose physically, to contest with.
definitions.example: My grandfather fought the Nazis in World War II.

definitions.definition: To try to overpower; to fiercely counteract.
definitions.example: The government pledged to fight corruption.

definitions.definition: To cause to fight; to manage or manoeuvre in a fight.
definitions.example: to fight cocks; to fight one's ship

definitions.definition: Of colours or other design elements: to clash; to fail to harmonize.
An occasion of fighting.
definitions.example: One of them got stuck in a chokehold and got stabbed to death during the fight.

definitions.definition: A battle between opposing armies.
A physical confrontation or combat between two or more people or groups.
definitions.example: Watch your language, are you looking for a fight?

definitions.definition: A boxing or martial arts match.
definitions.example: I'm going to Nick’s to watch the big fight tomorrow night.

definitions.definition: A conflict, possibly nonphysical, with opposing ideas or forces; strife.
definitions.example: I'll put up a fight to save this company.

definitions.definition: The will or ability to fight.
definitions.example: That little guy has a bit of fight in him after all.   As soon as he saw the size of his opponent, all the fight went out of him.

definitions.definition: A screen for the combatants in ships.
A large, undeveloped, humid forest, especially in a tropical region, that is home to many wild plants and animals; a tropical rainforest.
Any uncultivated tract of forest or scrub habitat.
A place where people behave ruthlessly, unconstrained by law or morality.
definitions.example: It’s a jungle out there.

definitions.definition: An area where hobos camp together.
A migrant camp.
A style of electronic music related to drum and bass.
(Israel) A desert region.
Dense rough.
A hairy vulva.
The short thick digit of the hand that for humans has the most mobility and can be made to oppose (moved to touch) all of the other fingers.
The part of a slider that may be moved linearly along the slider.
A thumbnail picture.
definitions.example: 2001, "Gary", Wanna See Porn? Take a Look At These (Free Expandable Thumbs) - CLICK HERE (on newsgroup alt.sex.services)

definitions.definition: To touch or cover with the thumb.
definitions.example: to thumb the touch-hole of a cannon

definitions.definition: (with through) To turn the pages of (a book) in order to read it cursorily.
definitions.example: I thumbed through the book and decided not to bother reading it all.

definitions.definition: (travel) To hitchhike
definitions.example: So I started thumbin' back east, toward my hometown.

definitions.definition: To soil or wear with the thumb or the fingers; to soil, or wear out, by frequent handling.
To manipulate (an object) with the thumb; especially, to pull back the hammer or open the cylinder of a revolver.
To fire (a single action revolver) quickly by pulling the hammer while keeping the trigger depressed.
The state of being tranquil
The absence of disturbance; peacefulness
The absence of stress; serenity
The quality of calm such as that experienced in places with mainly natural features and activities, free from disturbance from manmade ones.
To take a risk, with the potential of a positive outcome.
To play risky games, especially casino games, for monetary gain.
To risk (something) for potential gain.
definitions.example: He gambled his reputation on the outcome.

definitions.definition: To interact with equipment at a casino
An activity characterised by a balance between winning and losing that is governed by a mixture of skill and chance, usually with money wagered on the outcome.
A drinking vessel with a foot and stem.
A kind of small horse.
The state of being loyal; fidelity.
Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.
A device that can capture (receive) the signal sent over radio waves and render the modulated signal as sound.
On-board entertainment system in a car, usually including a radio receiver as well as the capability to play audio from recorded media.
A device that can transmit radio signals.
The continuous broadcasting of sound via the Internet in the style of traditional radio.
To use two-way radio to transmit (a message) (to another radio or other radio operator).
definitions.example: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.

definitions.definition: To order or assist (to a location), using telecommunications.
One of the main three types used for the Latin alphabet (the others being italics and blackletter), in which the ascenders are mostly straight.
A novel.
(of type) upright, as opposed to italic
(of text) of or related to the Latin alphabet
Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.
(more specific) A domestic animal, especially a bovine farm animal.
A person who behaves in a violent, antisocial or uncivilized manner.
Anything regarded as larger or more powerful than one of its normal size or strength.
definitions.example: That is a beast of a stadium.

definitions.definition: Someone who is particularly impressive, especially athletically or physically.
(prisons) A sex offender.
Something unpleasant and difficult.
A thing or matter, especially a difficult or unruly one.
To impose arduous exercises, either as training or as punishment.
(chiefly Midwestern and northeastern US) great; excellent; powerful
Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
Any of several small marine crustacean species of plankton in the order Euphausiacea in the class Malacostraca.
Any of various birds of the suborder Alcedines (or the family Alcedinidae sensu lato), having a large head, short tail and brilliant colouration; they feed mostly on fish.
A chemical element (symbol Sb, from Latin stibium) with an atomic number of 51: a lustrous gray metalloid.
The alloy stibnite.
A helpful action or an act of giving.
definitions.example: The foundation gave a much needed assist to the shelter.

definitions.definition: The act of helping another player score points or goals
definitions.example: He had two assists in the game.

definitions.definition: To help.
definitions.example: This book will assist you in getting your life in order.

definitions.definition: To make a pass that leads directly towards scoring.
To help compensate for what is missing with the help of a medical technique or therapy.
To stand (at a place) or to (an opinion).
definitions.example: A great part of the nobility assisted to his opinion.

definitions.definition: To be present (at an event, occasion etc.).
To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
definitions.example: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.

definitions.definition: To "walk free", i.e. to win, or avoid, a criminal court case, particularly when actually guilty.
definitions.example: If you can’t present a better case, that robber is going to walk.

definitions.definition: Of an object, to go missing or be stolen.
definitions.example: If you leave your wallet lying around, it’s going to walk.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To walk off the field, as if given out, after the fielding side appeals and before the umpire has ruled; done as a matter of sportsmanship when the batsman believes he is out.
To travel (a distance) by walking.
definitions.example: I walk two miles to school every day.  The museum’s not far from here – you can walk it.

definitions.definition: To take for a walk or accompany on a walk.
definitions.example: I walk the dog every morning.  Will you walk me home?

definitions.definition: To allow a batter to reach base by pitching four balls.
To move something by shifting between two positions, as if it were walking.
definitions.example: I carefully walked the ladder along the wall.

definitions.definition: To full; to beat cloth to give it the consistency of felt.
To traverse by walking (or analogous gradual movement).
definitions.example: I walked the streets aimlessly.   Debugging this computer program involved walking the heap.

definitions.definition: To operate the left and right throttles of (an aircraft) in alternation.
To leave, resign.
definitions.example: If we don't offer him more money he'll walk.

definitions.definition: To push (a vehicle) alongside oneself as one walks.
To behave; to pursue a course of life; to conduct oneself.
To be stirring; to be abroad; to go restlessly about; said of things or persons expected to remain quiet, such as a sleeping person, or the spirit of a dead person.
To be in motion; to act; to move.
To put, keep, or train (a puppy) in a walk, or training area for dogfighting.
(hotel) To move a guest to another hotel if their confirmed reservation is not available on day of check-in.
Incarnate as a human; living.
definitions.example: Elizabeth knows so many words that they call her the walking dictionary.

definitions.definition: Able to walk in spite of injury or sickness.
Characterized by or suitable for walking.
definitions.example: a walking tour
